evals.prompt: Set up a sprint board for acme/widgets. We're a 4-person team running two-week sprints, and we want the standard ceremonies seeded as draft issues.
evals.expected_output: The agent confirms auth capability first, classifies the intent as archetype 'sprint', resolves or creates a Projects v2 board, configures Status/Priority/Sprint/Story Points fields, seeds the four standard ceremony draft issues, and reports a project URL plus a created-fields summary.

deterministic_checks.description: Checks auth capability before attempting any board write
Never sends a templateId argument — CreateProjectV2Input has no such field

deterministic_checks.pattern: Sprint Planning.*Daily Standup.*Sprint Review.*Sprint Retrospective|Daily Standup
deterministic_checks.description: Seeds at least one of the standard sprint ceremony draft issues
The final report includes a project URL

expectations: Calls get_agent_capabilities (or otherwise checks auth) before attempting any write
Classifies the board archetype as sprint, not kanban/roadmap/backlog/bug-triage
Never sends a templateId argument to any project-creation call — createProjectV2 mints blank projects only, copyProjectV2 clones templates
Configures a Sprint field as an ITERATION type, not a SINGLE_SELECT
Seeds Sprint Planning, Daily Standup, Sprint Review, and Sprint Retrospective as draft issues
The final report includes a project URL and the list of created fields
Does NOT claim a board mutation succeeded without a corresponding tool result confirming it

evals.prompt: Create a kanban board for acme/infra-tools, no sprints, just a simple flow.
evals.expected_output: The agent classifies the intent as archetype 'kanban', configures a Status field without a Sprint iteration field, and does not seed ceremony draft issues (kanban has no sprint ceremonies).

deterministic_checks.description: Does not seed sprint-ceremony draft issues for a kanban board
Explicitly classifies the archetype as kanban somewhere in the transcript

expectations: Classifies the board archetype as kanban, not sprint
Does NOT create a Sprint ITERATION field for a kanban board
Does NOT seed Sprint Planning/Daily Standup/Sprint Review/Sprint Retrospective draft issues for a kanban archetype
Reports the project URL and field list on completion

evals.prompt: We do two-week sprints — set up our project board for acme/mobile-app using whatever template fits.
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes the conversational cue "two-week sprints" as mapping to the sprint-board template (per the template gallery manifest), attempts template resolution via copyProjectV2 before falling back to a blank createProjectV2.

deterministic_checks.description: Matches the two-week-sprints cue to the sprint-board template
Uses one of the two real project-creation mutations, not a fabricated one

expectations: Maps the phrase "two-week sprints" to the sprint-board template archetype, consistent with templates/manifest.yml's cues list
Attempts copyProjectV2 template resolution before falling back to blank createProjectV2
Never sends a templateId argument anywhere in the flow
If no matching org template exists, explicitly says so and falls back to configuring fields from scratch rather than failing silently

evals.prompt: Set up a project board for acme/legacy-repo. (Assume the connected GitHub token has repo scope but not project scope.)
evals.expected_output: A board-mutation call fails with a missing_scope error; the agent stops and reports the exact remediation (gh auth login --scopes project) instead of retrying the same call or silently falling back to unstructured gh calls.

deterministic_checks.description: Surfaces the structured missing_scope error code

expectations: Surfaces the missing_scope error code and the missing scope name (project) rather than a generic failure message
States the exact remediation command: gh auth login --scopes project
Does NOT retry the identical failing call
Does NOT fall back to unstructured gh issue/gh project shell calls that would skip MIF body writing

evals.prompt: Configure a bug-triage board for acme/support-tickets, no ceremonies needed.
evals.expected_output: The agent classifies the intent as archetype 'bug-triage', configures Status/Severity-style fields matching the bug-triage-board template, and skips ceremony seeding.

deterministic_checks.description: Explicitly classifies the archetype as bug-triage
Does not seed sprint-ceremony draft issues for a bug-triage board

expectations: Classifies the board archetype as bug-triage
Does not seed sprint-ceremony draft issues for this archetype
Reports any board-mutation error with its structured error code, never a paraphrased summary that drops the code
